data.content:
HANDOFF CAPSULE — foolery-a401

## Why this existed

The user previously thought this was fixed. It was not.
`src/lib/agent-session-runtime.ts` exposed `onResult` but gated
it on `obj.type === "result"` in the GENERIC runtime core
(old line 204). Every transport except Claude stream-json emits
a different terminator:
  - Codex jsonrpc: `{type: "turn.completed"}`
  - Gemini ACP:    ACP translator emits `{type: "result"}` only
                   when it resolves a `stopReason`
  - OpenCode HTTP: injected `{type: "step_finish"}`
So for 3 of 4 transports, `onResult` could never fire, which is
the root of the mysterious 557d-style silent terminations.

## Where the payload gate used to live (BEFORE this commit)

- `src/lib/agent-session-runtime.ts` line 204 (main @ f781a08e):
    `if (obj.type === "result") {
       handleResultEvent(child, state, config, obj);
     }`
  inside `processNormalizedEvent`, which ran on ALL transports.

## Where the turn-ended signal now lives (AFTER this commit)

Rename: `onResult` → `onTurnEnded`, `result_observed` →
`turn_ended` (both in SessionExitReason and the lifecycle event
union). All gates live in the transport adapters, not the core.

- stdio  (Claude, Copilot): `src/lib/agent-session-runtime-events.ts`
  `processStdioLine` at lines 205-222 gates on
  `obj.type === "result"` BEFORE calling `signal()`.
- jsonrpc (Codex):         `src/lib/agent-session-runtime-events.ts`
  `handleJsonRpcLine` at lines 132-156 gates on
  `turn.completed` / `turn.failed` AFTER the jsonrpc translator.
- acp    (Gemini):         `src/lib/agent-session-runtime-events.ts`
  `handleAcpLine` at lines 158-173 gates on the ACP translator's
  emitted `{type: "result"}` (which only appears when `stopReason`
  has resolved).
- http   (OpenCode):       also flows through `processStdioLine`
  because OpenCode HTTP inject lines are already translated to
  `{type: "step_finish"}` by the `translatePart` helper in
  `src/lib/opencode-http-session.ts` (lines 144-170), and the
  OpenCode normalizer maps `step_finish` -> `{type: "result"}`.

The transport-neutral dispatcher is `runtime.signalTurnEnded`
declared at `src/lib/agent-session-runtime-types.ts` lines
170-178 and implemented inside `createSessionRuntime` at
`src/lib/agent-session-runtime.ts` lines 220-222 (via the
closed-over `signal` helper). `handleTurnEnded` itself lives at
`src/lib/agent-session-runtime.ts` lines 52-72.

## Four new "onTurnEnded fires for transport X" tests

File: `src/lib/__tests__/on-turn-ended-transports.test.ts`
  - stdio      (Claude):        lines 95-115 — "fires on {type:'result'}"
  - jsonrpc    (Codex happy):   lines 128-169 — "fires on turn/completed notification"
  - jsonrpc    (Codex failed):  lines 171-199 — "fires on turn.failed (error path)"
  - acp        (Gemini):        lines 205-232 — "fires when prompt response resolves"
  - http       (OpenCode):      lines 238-260 — "fires on injected step_finish event"

## Canary tests against future fake-fixes

File: `src/lib/__tests__/on-turn-ended-transports.test.ts`
  - "does NOT fire on Codex item.completed" (lines 268-297)
  - "does NOT fire on stdio non-result messages" (lines 299-315)
  - "does NOT fire on opencode text deltas" (lines 317-333)
  - "runtime.signalTurnEnded fires regardless of payload"
    (lines 339-358) — locks the transport-neutral dispatcher

## STOP sign for future contributors

If you are tempted to add `if (obj.type === ...) { onTurnEnded() }`
anywhere in `src/lib/agent-session-runtime.ts`, STOP. That is the
exact fake-fix this knot eradicates. The payload gates belong in
`agent-session-runtime-events.ts` next to the transport adapter
that actually understands that payload shape.

## Also renamed for consistency

- `resultFollowUp` -> `turnEndedFollowUp` in
  `src/lib/terminal-manager-initial-io.ts`
- `recordTakeLoopLifecycle` now accepts `"turn_ended"` instead
  of `"result_observed"` in
  `src/lib/terminal-manager-take-lifecycle.ts` and
  `src/lib/terminal-manager-runtime-lifecycle.ts`

## Canary test NOT touched

`src/lib/__tests__/terminal-manager-watchdog-e2e.test.ts` (the
foolery-f8ae canary) did not need renaming — it only asserts on
`exitReason="timeout"`, not the turn-ended reason. No silencing
was required; the test still passes.

## Commit

5063357b60121fe9a2ba683a34f106f0b894f6ba on origin/main
